chapter_title.ar: باب شَهَادَةِ الْوَاحِدِ وَ يَمِينِ الْمُدَّعِي
chapter_title.en: Chapter 8 – The one testimony and the oath of the claimant

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (24/229)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (24/229)
text: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) يَقُولُ كَانَ عَلِيٌّ ( عليه السلام ) يُجِيزُ فِي الدَّيْنِ شَهَادَةَ رَجُلٍ وَ يَمِينَ الْمُدَّعِي .

en.hubeali: Al Husayn Bin Muhammad, from Moala Bin Muhammad, from Al Hassan Bin Ali Al Washa, from Hammad Bin Usman who said,
‘I heard Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> Saying; ‘Ali<sup>asws</sup> used to allow, with regards to the debt, the testimony of a man and an oath of the claimant’.
en.sarwar: 1. Al-Husayn Muhammad has narrated from Mu‘alla’ ibn Muhammad from al-Hassan ibn Ali al-Washsha’ from Hammad ibn ‘Uthaman who has narrated the following: “I once heard abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, saying that in the case of a loan ’ Amir al-Mu’minin, Ali, Alayhi al-Salam, would accept the testimony of one person with the oath of the plaintiff.”’
